signals.headline: South Korea March 2026 CPI Inflation Confirmed at 2
signals.raw:
South Korea March 2026 CPI Inflation Confirmed at 2.16% YoY | YES | HIGH confidence**

South Korea's annual consumer price inflation rose to 2.16% year-over-year in March 2026, up from 2.00% in February, with month-over-month inflation at 0.34%.[2] This follows steady 2.0% YoY headline CPI in February, where core inflation (excluding food and energy) increased to 2.3%, amid rising oil prices, KRW
matched_markets: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 2.7% and 2.9%?

signals.headline: Weekend talks in Islamabad ended without a deal, but both sides are now discussing a second round of negotiations before
signals.raw: Weekend talks in Islamabad ended without a deal, but both sides are now discussing a second round of negotiations before the two-week ceasefire—announced April 7—expires early next week[1][2].

signals.headline: Solana Hits All-Time Record 167 Million Monthly Token Holders & 148 Million Daily Non-Vote Transactions on April 14, 202
signals.raw:
Solana Hits All-Time Record 167 Million Monthly Token Holders & 148 Million Daily Non-Vote Transactions on April 14, 2026 | YES | HIGH confidence**  
This breaking network activity milestone, reported today, underscores surging adoption and performance, coinciding with a 10% SOL price surge amid anticipation for the Alpenglow upgrade targeting 150ms transaction finality.[1]

signals.raw:

signals.headline: **No major resolution**: Markets unchanged as talks loom but blockade persists without breakthrough
signals.raw: **No major resolution**: Markets unchanged as talks loom but blockade persists without breakthrough. Confidence medium due to consistent reporting from Bloomberg and Fox but reliance on unnamed sources and no official statements[1][2].

signals.headline: Solana Stablecoin Supply Reaches Record $16
signals.raw:
Solana Stablecoin Supply Reaches Record $16.3 Billion, Overtaking Ethereum in Adjusted Volume for Second Time | YES | HIGH confidence**  
DefiLlama data confirms a +10.87% weekly increase to $16.288 billion—the fastest growth among top chains—while Solana captured 32.6% of weekly adjusted stablecoin volume vs. Ethereum's 27.8% through April 12, signaling structural DeFi shift.[4]
